title: Vacant City Hall Commons in Downtown Syracuse could be converted to housing
summary: Holmes King Kallquist & Associates, a Syracuse architecture firm, proposes converting the vacant seven-story City Hall Commons building (built 1869) in Hanover Square into 39 mixed-use units featuring a restaurant anchor tenant and residential space. The Syracuse Landmark Preservation Board responded positively to the proposal on November 20, 2025, with further discussions planned. This project addresses downtown vacant property reactivation and historic preservation priorities.
department_refs: Department of Landmarks and Historic Preservation

political_signal.description: Article indicates alignment with downtown revitalization and historic preservation priorities. Board receptiveness signals administrative support for adaptive reuse of vacant municipal assets.
